violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment (ice machine) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of by person in charge. Ice machine was washed/rinsed/sanitized at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the speed racks used to store sprinkles and baked goods observed with rodent droppings. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

The floors in the bakers and front line area has an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Staff to implement daily cleaning logs that are signed off by management to ensure all sanitation practices are completed. Logs to be sent to inspector weekly. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on the side of speed racks and on the floors in the front service area and in the front dining area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. - Establishment to routinely sanitize and monitor daily for pest activity. All food and non-food contact surfaces to be cleaned and sanitized before closing. Before opening staff shall inspect affected areas and monitor as needed. All activity to be recorded in pest sighting logs. Pest control visits recommended to increase bi-weekly Failure to comply with all the following mentioned may result in disciplinary action. Establishment to submit plan of action by end of day 5.7.24. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back prep area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on the side of speed racks and on the floors throughout of the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. - Establishment to routinely sanitize and monitor daily for pest activity. All food and non-food contact surfaces to be cleaned and sanitized before closing. Before opening staff shall inspect affected areas and monitor as needed. All activity to be recorded in pest sighting logs. Pest control visits recommended to increase bi-weekly Failure to comply with all the following mentioned may result in disciplinary action. Person in charge to send plan of action to inspector by 4.26.24. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024

A rodent carcass was found in the back storage area. All trapped rodents shall be removed from traps to prohibit the accumulation of rodent caracasses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back prep area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on the side of speed racks and on the floors throughout of the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment to routinely sanitize and monitor daily for pest activity. All food and non-food contact surfaces to be cleaned and sanitized before closing. Before opening staff shall inspect affected areas and monitor as needed. All activity to be recorded in pest sighting logs. Pest control recommeneded to service location before next inspection. Failure to comply with all the following mentioned may result in disciplinary action. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
